sound_type: Loud directional biosonar -- sperm whale echolocation click train, 230-236 dB source level, the loudest biological sound on Earth, directional forward beam, giant squid hunting at 2,000+ meters depth, E=7
ecosystem: Deep water -- open ocean mesopelagic and bathypelagic; deep-diving predator
season: Rare (sperm whale presence in NE Pacific deep waters; uncommon in PNW-adjacent observations)
milestone: FIRST PHYSETERIDAE at degree 280. Physeter macrocephalus: largest toothed animal on Earth (male up to 57 metric tons). Click train 230-236 dB: loudest biological sound on Earth. E=7 because directional (not broadcast); power realized in forward beam. Combined E=15 with The Gits (S36 280, E=8).

coordinates.description: NE Pacific deep water. Sperm whales are found in deep ocean globally. NE Pacific distribution includes waters off PNW coast at depth. Deep diving species not regularly observed nearshore.

click_data.source_level_db: 230-236 dB re 1 µPa @ 1m. Mohl et al. (2003) confirmed. Highest source level measured for any biological sound. Exceeds orca echolocation (224 dB) by 6-12 dB (factor of ~4-16x acoustic pressure).
click_data.directionality: Narrow forward beam produced by spermaceti organ in head. NOT broadcast broadly -- aimed. An observer off the beam axis hears a much less intense sound. E=7 reflects this directionality.
click_data.frequency: Multi-kHz clicks, broadly distributed in frequency. Primary energy at several kHz. Individual clicks are broadband, short duration.
click_data.click_structure: Complex multi-pulse event. Primary click followed by regularly spaced secondary pulses (P1, P2, P3...) = reflections within spermaceti organ. Inter-pulse interval proportional to spermaceti organ size = individual acoustic fingerprint.
click_data.click_rate: Regular click train during hunting. Rate accelerates to 'creak' (rapid burst) during prey approach. Creak = acoustic signature of imminent prey capture.
click_data.e7_rationale: E=7 because directional, not broadcast. 230+ dB only in forward beam direction. Full acoustic power realized only on beam axis. Broadcast events (breach at E=9) outrank directional events at E=7 regardless of source level maximum.
click_data.prey: Giant squid (Architeuthis dux) and colossal squid (Mesonychoteuthis hamiltoni) at 2,000+ meters depth. Hunt entirely in acoustic space, in absolute darkness, at crushing pressure.

nodes.title: First Physeteridae -- Sperm Whale Family Enters
nodes.description: Degree 280 is the first entry in Physeteridae. The sperm whale: sole member of genus Physeter, largest toothed animal, 230+ dB directional biosonar. The engine has now documented Mysticeti (Balaenopteridae: 278-279) and Odontoceti (Physeteridae: 280) in consecutive family entries.

nodes.title: Loudest Biological Sound on Earth
nodes.description: 230-236 dB source level (Mohl et al. 2003). Highest measured for any biological sound. Directional, not broadcast. E=7 for directionality. Exceeds orca echolocation (224 dB) but is aimed rather than broadcast.

nodes.title: Deep Dive -- Hunt at 2,000+ Meters
nodes.description: Sperm whale dives to 2,000-3,000+ meters. Hunts giant squid and colossal squid at depth never directly observed by humans. Entire hunt in acoustic space. Click train at 230+ dB illuminates the dark deep ocean. Sucker scars on skin: evidence of battles no human has seen.

cross_references.relationship: Second Balaenopteridae then first Physeteridae. Two great whale families in consecutive degrees. Mysticeti (baleen) vs. Odontoceti (toothed). Low-frequency long-range (blue whale) vs. high-frequency directional biosonar (sperm whale).

224 dB orca vs. 230+ dB sperm whale. Both Odontoceti, both directional biosonar. Sperm whale more powerful; orca more frequency-agile and faster click rate. E=8 (orca) vs. E=7 (sperm whale) because of context and deployment.
Orca breach E=9 despite lower source level than sperm whale click. Reason: broadcast vs. directional. E reflects broadcast intensity. The E designation is not a decibel meter.

Combined E=15. First Physeteridae alongside the most direct, raw punk band in the Seattle Central District. Mia Zapata sending signals from the stage; sperm whale sending 230+ dB clicks into the deep ocean. Both targeting in the dark.
